notes: Page exists; covers vitamin D supplementation, not sunlight exposure specifically; relevant as mechanistic reference for vitamin D synthesis via UVB — keep as supporting ref

Gabel et al. 2022, J Affective Disorders; UK Biobank, 400,000+ participants; time in outdoor light associated with better mood, sleep, circadian timing, and lower depression/self-harm risk; large-scale population evidence
Blume et al. 2019, EXCLI Journal; narrative review of light effects on circadian rhythms, sleep, and mood; mechanism-level detail on morning light timing and phase-shifting
Luo et al. 2023; sunlight exposure and mental health in population without sunlight at work; confirms mood and wellbeing associations; occupational context useful
